name: Orlandi Valuta
description_short: Orlandi Valuta operates as a Western Union agent network offering money transfers, check cashing, and bill payment services across multiple U.S. locations.
description_long:
Orlandi Valuta is a money services company that functions primarily as a Western Union agent, providing access to international and domestic money transfer services through a network of physical locations across the United States. The company operates under the Orlandi Valuta brand but leverages Western Union's established infrastructure and technology platform for its core operations.

The company offers a range of financial services centered on money movement and cash access. Primary services include sending money domestically and internationally, receiving money transfers, picking up cash from Western Union transfers, tracking transfers in real-time, paying bills, and accessing prepaid card services. Customers can also enroll in Western Union Rewards, use mobile top-up services, and access inmate payment services. The platform supports multiple languages including English, Spanish, Haitian Creole, Chinese, French, and Vietnamese, reflecting a diverse customer base.

Orlandi Valuta distinguishes itself through its agent-based network model, partnering with local businesses in underserved communities rather than maintaining company-owned locations. The sample locations shown include check cashing shops, specialty stores, and small businesses in Colorado and New Mexico, suggesting a strategy of embedding financial services in existing community retail establishments. This approach provides convenient access to financial services in areas that may be underbanked.

As a Western Union agent operating check-cashing services, Orlandi Valuta serves customers who need fast access to cash and international money transfer capabilities but may not have traditional bank accounts. However, customers should be aware that fees apply to all transactions, pricing varies by service and location, and this is not a bank—deposits are not FDIC-insured. The reliance on third-party Western Union technology and fee structures means customers have limited control over pricing.
address: 1250 Pioneer Pkwy Suite Envios Hispanos 2, Arlington, TX 76010

company_info.headquarters: 1250 Pioneer Pkwy Suite Envios Hispanos 2, Arlington, TX 76010

pros: Multi-language support including English, Spanish, Haitian Creole, Chinese, French, and Vietnamese for diverse customer populations
Access to Western Union's established global money transfer network for sending and receiving international funds
Multiple convenient services available at single locations: money transfers, check cashing, bill payment, and mobile top-up
Online account management with ability to track transfers, view history, and manage receivers from any device
Agent network model provides financial services access in underserved communities with partner local businesses
Extended service hours at many locations (some open as early as 8:00 AM)
Western Union Rewards program loyalty benefits for repeat customers
cons: All transactions subject to Western Union fees, which can be substantial for international transfers and are non-negotiable
Limited control over pricing structure as an agent—pricing determined by Western Union headquarters, not Orlandi Valuta
Sample location data shows only 7 active locations across Colorado and New Mexico, suggesting limited geographic coverage
No FDIC deposit protection or bank-account functionality—purely transactional service provider
Dependent on Western Union's technology platform and policies; customer service routing goes through Western Union (800 number shown)
best_for: Immigrants and international families needing regular money transfers to relatives abroad
Unbanked and underbanked consumers in rural or underserved communities with limited bank access
Consumers without traditional bank accounts who need check cashing and bill payment services
Customers requiring multilingual financial services in their native language
diagnosis: Orlandi Valuta is best for immigrants, unbanked consumers, and individuals without traditional bank accounts who need reliable money transfer and check cashing services in underserved communities. The main caveat is that this is a Western Union agent network, not an independent financial institution—all services carry Western Union's standard fees, and customers have no ability to negotiate pricing or control transaction costs.
services: Money pickup from Western Union transfers
Western Union Rewards loyalty program enrollment
Money receiver management and contact list
